slides.title: Business Overview
bullets: Aug–Sep 2023 drove $1.84M (19.8% of annual revenue) — strongest stretch of the year
Feb 2023 was the weakest month at $651K — 31% below the August peak
Churned customers represent 26.5% of revenue ($2.47M) — highest of any segment, signaling retention risk

slides.title: Monthly Revenue Trend
slides.notes: Revenue peaked in Aug ($942K) and dipped in Feb ($651K). Q4 recovery strong with Nov–Dec averaging $856K.

slides.title: Revenue Breakdown
slides.notes: Categories are evenly distributed (11–14% each). North America and Latin America are near-tied at ~22% each.

slides.title: Sales Channels & Customer Segments
slides.notes: Marketplace leads channels at 27.8%. Churned segment at 26.5% is the largest — a key retention opportunity.

slides.title: Top Products & Payment Trends
bullets: Top 3 products (Sneakers, LEGO Set, Cookbook) account for $930K — 10% of total revenue
Crypto is the #1 payment method at 23.1% ($2.15M), followed closely by Credit Card (21.7%)
Buy Now Pay Later is the smallest share at 15.9% ($1.48M) — room to grow with flexible payment adoption
slides.right_notes:
Payment Mix:
Crypto 23.1%
Credit Card 21.7%
Bank Transfer 20.9%
PayPal 18.5%
BNPL 15.9%

slides.title: Key Takeaways & Next Steps
takeaways: Revenue is well-diversified across all 8 categories (11–14% each) — no single category dependency
North America and Latin America are the top two regions, contributing 44.6% of revenue combined
Churned customers are the largest segment at 26.5% — retention programs could recover significant revenue
Aug–Sep peak ($1.84M) and Q4 strong close ($1.71M) suggest seasonal demand patterns worth planning for
5.85% return rate and average rating of 3.03/5 indicate product quality and fulfillment improvements are needed
next_steps: Launch win-back campaigns targeting the Churned segment ($2.47M at risk)
Investigate low customer rating (3.03/5) — identify top complaint categories and address quality gaps
Expand Marketplace channel investment — already #1 at 27.8% and growing
Plan inventory and promotions ahead of Aug–Sep to maximize peak season revenue
